title: clematis viorna var. parviflora
intro: Clematis viorna var. parviflora, also known as Woodbine or Wild Clematis, is a perennial vine native to North America, characterized by its clusters of small, fragrant white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which are derived from its alkaloids and flavonoids. Consuming clematis extracts has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting immune function, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, clematis is often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are believed to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and aid in the treatment of various ailments.
